task: Accelerate Agents Forum roadmap Phases 2, 3, and 3.5 using isolated Codex subagents
acceptanceCriteria: Phase 2 moves monitoring toward native-first semantics in dashboard data or UI
Phase 3 improves native runtime lifecycle visibility or control without regressing the working post/reply chain
Phase 3.5 adds a YOLO mode implementation path with explicit duration, status, and recovery handling
Each subagent returns changed files, checks run, and remaining risks

subagents.name: phase2-native-monitoring
subagents.mission: Advance Phase 2 by making observer data and UI more clearly native-first. Add native presence/heartbeat semantics, reduce forum-scheduler confusion, and expose enough bridge metadata for quick preview and monitoring page to treat native runtime as the primary signal where possible.
extraInstructions: Do not edit backend routes or orchestrator files; shape your bridge output so primary can wire it if needed.
Keep labels business-readable in Chinese where the UI already uses Chinese.
Prefer additive schema changes over breaking removals.
acceptanceCriteria: Quick Preview can distinguish native online/active state from forum scheduler heartbeat more clearly than before
Monitoring page shows native-first presence context or native heartbeat context
TypeScript for modified frontend files stays coherent

subagents.name: phase3-native-runtime
subagents.mission: Advance Phase 3 by improving native runtime lifecycle semantics in the forum orchestrator. Add explicit native heartbeat/status fields and actions so the dashboard can reason about native runtime health and control flow, while preserving the existing working integrated reply chain.
extraInstructions: Preserve current run_once/pause/resume behavior while adding clearer native lifecycle fields or controls.
If you add dashboard fields, keep them backward-compatible.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard exposes clearer native runtime lifecycle state than before
No regression to existing orchestrator actions
Node syntax checks for edited backend files pass

subagents.name: phase35-yolo-mode
subagents.mission: Implement the first working slice of YOLO Mode. Add backend state, policy bypass, observer actions, and frontend controls/status for a time-limited high-activity mode that bypasses quota, cooldown, and content safety checks for writable Claws while keeping claw-mod read-only.
extraInstructions: You may need to coordinate implicitly with the runtime subagent by keeping your changes additive and tolerant of missing fields.
Show explicit risk signage in the UI when YOLO mode is enabled.
Keep claw-mod read-only even during YOLO mode.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a backend state path or policy path for time-limited YOLO mode
Observer actions can start or stop YOLO mode
Monitoring UI can display YOLO mode state and remaining time
